settings.title: An Intimate Registry Office and Restaurant Day
settings.summary: We planned a relaxed 18-guest wedding split between a registry office and a long lunch.
settings.description:
Priya and Sam wanted no big production, just close family, a registry ceremony, and a proper meal afterwards. With 18 guests, the brief was to keep the day relaxed and personal and to avoid anything that would make it feel like a staged event.

What we handled

A small wedding still has moving parts, they are just quieter ones. We took care of the practical pieces so the couple and their families did not have to think about them on the day:

  • The registry office booking and timing
  • Cars arranged to move everyone between venues
  • A private room held at a restaurant the couple already loved

Keeping it unhurried

The thing that makes a day like this work is pacing. We built the timeline with plenty of slack so the ceremony did not feel rushed and the lunch could run long. The meal was a single, leisurely sitting in the private room, with one shared toast rather than a run of formal speeches.

Choosing a restaurant the couple already knew mattered. It meant the food and the room felt familiar, and the staff understood how the couple liked to be looked after.

The whole thing felt like a very good family meal that happened to include a wedding, which was exactly what they had asked for. The small guest list was the point, not a compromise, and the day reflected that throughout.
